frontmatter_flags.notes: Land — no mana_cost field expected. blue-magic and red-magic present in tags_filter. flavor_text is absent from Scryfall API response (confirmed no flavor text on this card).

trivia_bullets.text: Swiftwater Cliffs originates from Khans of Tarkir (September 2014) as part of a 10-card common 'gain land' cycle covering all 10 allied and enemy color pairs. The cycle is a functional reprint of the Zendikar 'Refuge' lands (2009) expanded to all 10 color pairs. Swiftwater Cliffs covers blue-red (Izzet colors). [Scryfall: set=ktk, released_at=2014-09-26; WebSearch: MTG-Realm Tumblr, Khans of Tarkir Land Cycle]
sources: WebSearch: MTG-Realm Tumblr — Khans of Tarkir Land Cycle overview

trivia_bullets.text: Gain lands enter the battlefield tapped and provide 1 life on entry, sacrificing tempo for a minor life buffer. They are a Pauper format staple and among the most accessible dual-land options in budget Commander and lower-power formats. As of 2024, Swiftwater Cliffs has 22 total prints — one of the most-reprinted land cycles in the game. [Scryfall: prints search, total_cards=22; WebSearch: ScrollVault MTG Dual Lands Guide]
sources: WebSearch: ScrollVault 'MTG Dual Lands Guide — Every Cycle by Format'

trivia_bullets.text: The Eternal Masters printing carries art by Eytan Zana, depicting a sandstone canyon waterfall. Zana also illustrated the EMA printing of Wind-Scarred Crag (card 249 in the same set), a compositional pairing of dramatic wilderness landscapes across the two allied-adjacent gain lands in that set. No flavor text appears on this card in any printing. [Scryfall: artist=Eytan Zana]

related_cards.name: Wind-Scarred Crag
related_cards.note: same set, same artist (Eytan Zana), same gain-land cycle

related_cards.name: Sulfur Falls
related_cards.note: blue-red dual land, stronger alternative for the same color pair
